title: The Idiot's Guide to Winning Best Picture. Plus: We Make the Case for 'Parasite.' | The Oscars Show
format: Sean and Amanda run down the guild sweep for '1917,' take another crack at explaining the Academy's preferential ballot, and spend the back half building the case for 'Parasite' over the presumptive front-runner.

segments.heading: The Idiot's Guide to Winning Best Picture
segments.note: the guild recap and the preferential ballot

films.title: Jojo Rabbit
films.note: Taika Waititi beat Greta Gerwig for the WGA's adapted screenplay award, which Sean and Amanda read as the clearest signal yet that Gerwig won't win the Oscar. Both are lukewarm on the film itself.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: The screenplay they think should have beaten Jojo Rabbit.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Won the WGA for original screenplay and is the subject of the episode's second half.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Shut out of the WGAs since Tarantino isn't a member, which Sean says muddies its screenplay case at the Oscars.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Discussed as a once-presumed screenplay front-runner that's fallen off the map entirely, a shift Amanda compares to Mike Mills's '20th Century Women.'

films.title: 20th Century Women
films.note: Amanda's comparison for how 'Marriage Story' connected more with critics and podcast hosts than with a wider audience.

Won Best Picture and Best Director at the BAFTAs and has swept the Globes, PGA, and DGA, which the hosts call a boring, locked-in outcome given it's not competitive for editing, screenplay, or any acting award.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Amanda's running example for how the preferential ballot's elimination-and-redistribution math actually works.

films.title: Joker
films.note: Comes up as Amanda's last-place pick on her preferential ballot.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Both hosts rank it in their personal top five, ahead of 1917.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: Last year's preferential-ballot case study; its win convinced Amanda the film had broader top-three support than the 'divisive' narrative suggested.

films.title: Roma
films.note: The other 2019 Best Picture front-runner, cited alongside Green Book as neck-and-neck contenders that year.

segments.heading: Should the Academy Blow Up Best Picture?

films.title: The Dark Knight
films.note: Its 2009 Best Picture snub is why the Academy expanded the nominee field and brought back the preferential ballot in the first place.

films.title: The French Connection
films.note: Part of Sean's case that 1970s Best Picture winners are a stronger lineup than the 2000s'.

films.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
Star Wars
films.note: Sean's quibble: it maybe should have beaten Annie Hall.

films.title: Crash
films.note: One of the 2000s winners Sean and Amanda call a 'dog baby' outcome by comparison.

films.title: Argo
films.note: Sean likes it personally but questions whether it's an important movie.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: The win Sean hoped would permanently change the Academy's taste; he says the opposite happened.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Sean's joking punchline for where this argument about cinema's changing cultural role leads.

segments.heading: We Make the Case for Parasite

films.title: Parasite
films.note: Amanda has been on it since her first viewing. The hosts say it doesn't waste a moment, swinging from satire to horror to devastation within minutes, and that it holds up whether you watch it casually or pick it apart.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Sean's favorite film of the year, contrasted with Parasite as a fairy tale built on two unlikable leads made righteous by killing the Manson Family.
Its lead is virtuous and mission-bound by design; the hosts contrast that straightforward heroism with Parasite's morally messier Kim family.

films.title: The Host
films.note: Bong Joon-ho's monster movie that's also an ecological parable, part of the hosts' case that he's unusually conscious of his own genre commentary.

films.title: Mother
films.note: Cited for its fraught thriller premise about a mother protecting her family.

films.title: Snowpiercer
films.note: Amanda's wife loved this one on a rewatch binge after seeing Parasite; the hosts call it and Okja more absurdist and 'meta' than Parasite's grounded, human-scale story.

films.title: Okja
films.note: Same rewatch binge; Amanda wasn't its biggest fan on first watch.

films.title: Memories of Murder
films.note: Sean rewatched it recently on a rough streaming rip; Neon holds the rights and is reportedly planning a Blu-ray.

films.title: The Departed
films.note: Scorsese's only competitive Best Director win, which Sean calls a cool crime thriller but far from his best work, offered as a precedent for Bong potentially winning now for a film that isn't his 'best.'

films.title: Mean Streets
films.note: One of the Scorsese films Sean ranks above The Departed.

films.title: Blade Runner 2049
films.note: Sean's example of Vegas oddsmakers reliably nailing a below-the-line category like visual effects over the comic-book favorite.

films.title: Psycho
films.note: Sean's Hitchcock comparison for Bong, drawing a line from it to The Host.

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood

One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
